acceptance_criteria: The registration form accepts a valid email, password, and username.
An error is shown for invalid email.
Duplicate email registrations & usernames are rejected.
A successful registration logs the user in and confirms account creation.

benefit: access my personal dashboard and group functionalities
acceptance_criteria: The login form accepts correct credentials and denies incorrect ones.
A session is maintained until logout.
Error messages are displayed on failed login attempts.

benefit: see my personal details and stored data
acceptance_criteria: The profile page displays the user’s information, want-to-watch list, favorite genre, favorite film, and watched movie list.
Data loads correctly and errors are handled with sensible messages.
This can only be accessed by logged-in users.

benefit: see, edit, and append to my want-to-watch list
acceptance_criteria: One can only edit their own want-to-watch list.
Movies can be added to the watchlist.
Movies already on the watch list cannot be added.
The user can search with key words for movies.
The user can select genres from the UI to browse movies and then add them.
A new group is created when a valid group name is provided.
Valid group names are unique; if not, a similar one is suggested.
The group is associated with the creator’s account and visible in their group list.
Duplicate group names trigger an error.

benefit: include them in the group movie selection process
acceptance_criteria: Invited friends receive a request in the application, acceptance of which makes them members of the group.
Only usernames of registered users can be invited.

benefit: find films to add to the group pool
acceptance_criteria: A search query returns a list of movies with titles, posters, and brief details.
Invalid queries return a “no results found” message.
The search function responds within a reasonable time.
One can search for movie titles, directors, and actors.

goal: add a movie to the group pool
acceptance_criteria: A movie can be added by clicking an “Add” button on the search result.
The movie appears in the group’s pool immediately.
Duplicate entries are not allowed; an error message is displayed.
The pool displays all movies added with relevant details.
The UI updates when new movies are added.
The list is visible to all group members.

benefit: ensure my vote counts toward the final selection
acceptance_criteria: Each user can vote for one movie per movie night event.
The vote is registered and displayed in the UI.
Only the latest vote is counted and users can change their vote.
When the voting period ends, the system counts votes accurately.
The movie with the highest vote count is selected automatically.
The winning movie is clearly displayed.
In a tie scenario, the tiebreaker of highest rank is applied.
The tie-breaker process is logged and documented.
The final decision is displayed to all group members.

benefit: understand how the group arrived at the final selection
acceptance_criteria: The results page shows the (anonymous) vote count for each movie.
The selected movie is highlighted as the winner.
Results update in real time as votes are cast or changed.

benefit: ensure the integrity of the selection process
acceptance_criteria: Votes for movies not in the pool cannot be submitted.
Appropriate error messages are shown for invalid votes.
All valid votes are logged with user ID and timestamp.

goal: receive clear error messages and confirmations
benefit: know when my actions succeed or fail
acceptance_criteria: Confirmation messages appear upon successful registration, group creation, movie addition, and vote submission.
Error messages display for any invalid inputs (e.g., duplicate votes, invalid email formats).
The UI responds promptly to user actions with clear feedback.
Upon registration, the user is asked to provide their movie preferences.
The user can select favorite genres from a list.
The user can search through all movies and select one as their favorite.
The user can opt out of this process.
The profile page displays the user’s information, want-to-watch list, favorite genre, and favorite film.
Users can set their profile to private if they do not want to be found.
Users can be found by username if their profile is public.
Users can decide which information is public.
This feature is accessible only to logged-in users.

goal: manage my want-to-watch list with suggestions
benefit: receive tailored movie suggestions for my watchlist
acceptance_criteria: One can only edit their own want-to-watch list.
Movie suggestions are generated using multiple dimensions (genre, actors, popularity, directors, personal history, etc.).
Suggested movies can be added to the watchlist.
No movies already on the watchlist are suggested.
Suggested movies can be downvoted and won’t be suggested again.
Movies marked as watched are not suggested.
The discover movies page displays suggested movies in a Tinder-like style, with pictures and short descriptions.
A link to the trailer is provided.
If a user likes a movie, it is added to their want-to-watch list.
If a user downvotes a movie, it won’t be suggested again.
The user can swipe or click through the suggestions.

benefit: add new movies to the pool
acceptance_criteria: The suggested movies have not yet been seen by any of the group members.
The suggestions follow the movie suggestion algorithm.
Users can change their vote prior to the end of the voting period.
Only the latest vote is counted in the final tally.
The UI reflects the updated vote immediately.
In the already watched list, users can see a rating (1-5 stars) for each movie.
Users can edit these ratings.
Votes for a movie added by several users are multiplied by weights.
The password must be at least 12 characters long.
The password must contain at least one uppercase letter.
The password must contain at least one lowercase letter.
The password must contain at least one numeric digit.
The password must contain at least one special character (e.g., !@#$%^&*()).
